aplicacao.title: Application in Transportation Systems
aplicacao.description:
Rectifier Transformers

Epoxy resin cast dry-type rectifier transformers represent the ideal solution for applications demanding high reliability, thermal robustness and operational safety, particularly in environments where the use of oil is undesirable. Designed to power semiconductor rectifier circuits from 6 to 24 pulses, these transformers face more severe electrical conditions than those encountered in conventional transformers, due to the high-frequency harmonics generated by the AC–DC conversion process. The operation of semiconductor devices introduces harmonic components that significantly increase eddy current losses and stray losses, causing additional heating in the windings and metallic structures of the transformer. Given this scenario, the epoxy resin dry-type transformer must be conceived with a set of electromagnetic and thermal refinements to ensure stability and longevity. Among the main design elements adopted by Zilmer Ineltec are: reduction of magnetic flux density, minimising additional losses caused by harmonics; strict control of current density in conductors, preventing excessive heating under pulsating load conditions; electrostatic shielding between windings, attenuating high-frequency interference and protecting sensitive components of the rectifier system; windings encapsulated in Class F or H epoxy resin, which provide excellent thermal conductivity, high dielectric rigidity and superior resistance to contaminated, humid or corrosive atmospheres; a dry-type design that is inherently safer, eliminating risks associated with leaks, oil flammability and reducing maintenance requirements. These characteristics ensure that the dry-type rectifier transformer delivers consistent reliability and performance, even under the nonlinear demands typical of intensive industrial applications, such as electrolysis, variable-speed drives, electric furnaces, mining, traction systems and other processes requiring high-quality direct current.
aplicacao.heroDescription: Zilmer transformers are fundamental to metro rail transport infrastructure, providing reliable power for critical urban mobility systems.

aplicacao.title: Application in Hydropower Plants
aplicacao.description:
Excitation Transformer in Power Generation

At the heart of any electrical power generation system, the excitation transformer plays a fundamental and strategic role in system stability. As a vital component in the control of synchronous generators, it is responsible for converting and adjusting the voltage from the generator busbars or auxiliary services to power the excitation system. This process is what enables the creation of the magnetic field required in the rotor to induce voltage in the stator windings, converting mechanical energy into electricity available for the grid. At Zilmer, we understand that the efficiency of a power plant depends directly on the precision of this component, and we therefore develop excitation transformers designed to operate in harmony with the most modern Automatic Voltage Regulators.

Our engineering focuses on the robustness required to withstand the severe operational demands of the power sector. Unlike conventional transformers, Zilmer excitation models are dimensioned to withstand ceiling currents and rapid load variations, ensuring that the generator responds swiftly to grid disturbances and maintains stable output voltage. Manufactured with high-class insulation materials, our equipment offers maximum safety and low maintenance requirements — crucial factors for the operational continuity of hydropower and thermal power plants.
aplicacao.onde: Installed in powerhouses, generation substations and energy transmission systems.
aplicacao.como: They transform the energy generated by turbines to voltage levels appropriate for transmission and distribution.
aplicacao.heroDescription: High-power transformers essential for hydropower generation and transmission.

solucao.melhora: Our transformers offer high energy efficiency, reducing losses and maximising renewable energy production.

aplicacao.title: Application in Mining Operations
aplicacao.description:
High-Performance Transformers for Mining

In the mining industry, electromechanical reliability transcends operational objectives, establishing itself as a critical safety and continuity requirement. Aware of this reality, Zilmer develops power and distribution transformers with a rigorous focus on thermomechanical resilience, incorporating enclosures with an IP54 degree of protection — or higher, upon technical consultation. This construction standard ensures that the equipment is fully protected against the ingress of abrasive dust, preventing harmful deposits, and against water projections from any direction. These characteristics make Zilmer solutions ideal for harsh environments such as crushing, grinding and wet beneficiation plants, where conventional equipment would be subject to premature failure.

The technical development of Zilmer transformers for mining is based on advanced engineering criteria to mitigate risks inherent to the sector. The IP54 metallic enclosure is designed with ventilation systems equipped with special filters. This physical barrier prevents contamination of windings and insulators by conductive dust or pulverised ore, eliminating the risk of electrical tracking and internal arcing that could compromise the entire electrical network of the facility.

Even with the characteristic sealing of the IP54 enclosure, Zilmer guarantees superior thermal management through optimised cooling systems, such as ANAF (Natural Air / Forced Air). This technology ensures efficient heat dissipation, keeping the thermal insulation class strictly within the limits established by ABNT NBR standards, even in locations with elevated ambient temperatures. Furthermore, the internal structure of the core and coils receives reinforced mechanical locking, specifically dimensioned to withstand continuous vibrations and mechanical shocks from large-scale equipment such as crushers and vibrating screens installed in close proximity.

In this way, Zilmer delivers to the mining market a solution that combines hermetic environmental protection, thermal stability and structural robustness. The result is equipment with an extended service life, minimal maintenance requirements and the assurance that the operation will maintain its maximum productivity levels, protecting the client's investment and the integrity of the electrical plant.
aplicacao.onde: Installed in processing plants, extraction areas, ore transport systems and mine infrastructure.
aplicacao.como: They operate in environments with high humidity, dust, vibrations and extreme temperature variations.
aplicacao.heroDescription: Robust transformers designed for the extreme conditions of open-cast mining operations.

aplicacao.title: Application in Electrical Substations
aplicacao.description:
Substation Transformers

Within Zilmer's solutions ecosystem, the development of substation equipment is guided by high engineering standards and the need to ensure the stability and safety of the electrical system. Zilmer's product range spans from large-scale power transformation to critical operational support and system protection, distinguished by its robustness and technical efficiency.

Zilmer power transformers represent the central pillar of any substation. Designed to operate at the interface between transmission and distribution systems, these units perform voltage conversion with maximum performance and reduced losses. Built to withstand severe load conditions, Zilmer power transformers employ state-of-the-art insulation systems and optimised cooling, ensuring that energy is transported safely and reliably to meet the demands of utilities and large industrial plants.

To ensure system integrity in fault situations, Zilmer grounding reactors play a vital role in operational safety. These units are designed to create a neutral point in systems that lack one, or to limit ground fault current to safe values. Zilmer's technology in these reactors minimises damage caused by electric arcs and controls transient overvoltages, raising the standard of asset protection and ensuring more resilient operation in the event of grid disturbances.

Complementing the infrastructure, Zilmer's Auxiliary Services Transformer (AST) is the guarantee of autonomy and control. While the large power transformers handle the main load, the Zilmer AST diverts the power required to supply the substation's own internal systems, such as protection panels, battery chargers, automation systems and lighting. Developed with a focus on the highest reliability, Zilmer ASTs ensure that the substation maintains its monitoring and control capability under all circumstances, serving as the backbone that sustains the intelligence of the entire facility.
aplicacao.onde: Installed in distribution, transmission and power transformation substations.
aplicacao.como: They transform voltages between different levels, enabling efficient distribution of energy to consumers.
aplicacao.heroDescription: Essential transformers for energy distribution and transmission in substations.

aplicacao.title: Application in Renewable Energy
aplicacao.description:
Transformers for Renewable Sources

Zilmer's dry-type transformers for renewable energy sources are developed with state-of-the-art engineering to meet the critical demands of this type of generation, where the integration of photovoltaic and wind systems imposes severe load conditions due to the high injection of harmonics. Since power inverters operate through electronic switching, they generate nonlinear currents that distort the sinusoidal waveform, resulting in multiples of the fundamental frequency that cause excessive heating in conventional transformers. To address this challenge, Zilmer designs its equipment with a rigorously dimensioned K-Factor, ensuring that the transformer can withstand supplementary eddy current losses and stray losses without compromising its thermal integrity.

Unlike conventional models, the construction of these transformers focuses on mitigating the effects of harmonics through an optimised winding geometry, utilising conductors arranged to minimise the skin effect and reduce resistance at high frequencies. The use of vacuum-cast epoxy resin insulation gives the equipment self-extinguishing characteristics and high dielectric resistance, eliminating the risk of fires and the need for fluid maintenance — essential for the safety and sustainability of distributed generation plants. Furthermore, the inclusion of electrostatic shielding between windings acts as a filter against transients and electromagnetic noise, protecting sensitive inverter electronics and improving the quality of energy delivered to the grid.

From an operational standpoint, Zilmer transformers with an appropriate K-Factor eliminate the need for derating (underutilisation of nominal capacity), allowing the asset to operate at full capacity with maximum energy efficiency. The mechanical robustness and integrated temperature monitoring system ensure a long service life, even under the load fluctuations typical of renewable energy sources.
aplicacao.onde: Installed in wind farms, solar power plants and grid integration systems.
aplicacao.como: They connect renewable generation to the grid, transforming voltages and ensuring power quality.
aplicacao.heroDescription: Specialised transformers for the integration of wind and solar energy into the electrical grid.

solucao.melhora: Our transformers maximise conversion efficiency, reducing losses and optimising clean energy production.
solucao.essencial: Essential for the energy transition, enabling efficient integration of renewable sources into the power matrix.

aplicacao.title: Application in Control and Metering Systems
aplicacao.description:
Potential and Current Transformers for Control and Metering

Instrument Transformers play a fundamental role in electrical infrastructure, acting as critical interfaces between high-power systems and monitoring devices. In essence, their function is to reduce high electrical quantities — currents and voltages that can reach hundreds of kilovolts (kV) or kiloamperes (kA) — to standardised, safe levels compatible with measuring instruments, billing meters and protection relays. In addition to scale reduction, these devices provide galvanic isolation, physically separating the high-voltage circuit from the control panels, thus ensuring the integrity of electronic equipment and, above all, the safety of operators handling the instruments.

In this context, the use of equipment from established manufacturers such as Zilmer ensures that precision and robustness requirements are met in accordance with current technical standards (such as ABNT NBR and IEC). The Current Transformer, for example, is designed to be connected in series with the load, converting the primary circuit current into a proportional secondary current, generally standardised at 1 A or 5 A. In metering applications, the focus of the current transformer is on high precision within the nominal operating range to ensure fair energy billing. In control and protection applications, models manufactured by Zilmer are developed to operate without premature saturation under short-circuit conditions, enabling relays to identify severe faults clearly.

Conversely, the Potential Transformer operates analogously to a conventional power transformer, but with a focus on waveform fidelity and voltage magnitude. It is connected in parallel with the system and reduces the voltage to levels of 115 V or 120 V. Zilmer offers solutions in potential transformers that prioritise thermal stability and transformation ratio precision, which are fundamental for supplying voltage coils of meters and under/overvoltage protection systems.
aplicacao.onde: Installed in control panels, protection systems, meters and automation equipment.
aplicacao.como: They reduce voltages and currents to safe levels, enabling precise measurement and protection of electrical systems.
aplicacao.heroDescription: Instrument transformers (potential and current) for protection, control and metering in electrical installations.
